## Final Report — Fixed Asset Management System (env: `fable14232`)

### 1. Data Model

| Collection | Template | Key Fields | Relations |
|---|---|---|---|
| `asset_categories` | tree | name, description | parent/children (tree), → assets (reverse o2m) |
| `employees` | general | name, email, phone, department | → owned_assets (reverse o2m) |
| `assets` | general | asset_tag, name, purchase_date, purchase_value, current_value, depreciation_method (enum), useful_life_years, status (enum), location, description, depreciation_pct (formula) | category → asset_categories, owner → employees, → maintenance_records (reverse o2m) |
| `maintenance_records` | general | maintenance_date, maintenance_type (enum), cost, performed_by, description, next_maintenance_date | asset → assets |

### 2. Pages

| Page | schemaUid | Blocks |
|---|---|---|
| Asset Catalog | `n1ufjgrqj7z` | TreeBlock (asset_categories, left facet) + GridCardBlock (assets, connected via category.id, shows depreciation_pct) |
| Assets | `8opo29jx370` | TableBlock with filter/addNew/view/edit |
| Asset Categories | `xq46bgwb8m5` | TableBlock with filter/addNew/view/edit |
| Maintenance Records | `wuitrl62wnz` | TableBlock with filter/addNew/view/edit |

### 4. Known Incomplete / Design Trade-offs

**Design trade-offs:**
- Association fields named as nouns (`category`, `owner`, `asset`) per prompt requirement, NOT `<role>_id`
- Asset Catalog uses GridCardBlock (card grid) with tree facet; depreciation shown as `depreciation_pct` formula field (remaining value %)
- Detail views use drawer popups (not standalone pages)
- `asset_categories` uses tree template for hierarchical category navigation

**Things not done:**
- No Dashboard / charts / reports
- No ACL / role permissions
- No workflow / auto-notifications
- No import/export
- No i18n

**Known limitations:**
- Depreciation bar on cards shows as a numeric percentage field (formula `current_value / purchase_value * 100`); a visual progress bar would require JS custom rendering
- Standalone detail pages not built; all detail views are drawer popups

2. Open **Fixed Assets > Assets** — table with 7 assets. Use the filter to search by name/status. Click "View" on a row to see the detail drawer with category and owner popups.
3. Open **Fixed Assets > Maintenance Records** — table with 6 records across all 4 maintenance types. Click "View" to see the linked asset popup.
4. Open **Fixed Assets > Asset Categories** — tree-structured categories. The table shows parent-child relationships.
